stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags such as 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and utilization of a major LLM architecture.

stories.title: Nvidia bets $150B on Taiwan as Trump's plan to make US an AI hub backfires
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Nvidia is committing a massive $150 billion annual investment to Taiwan.
The investment aims to establish Taiwan as a global AI 'epicenter'.
The move is framed against the backdrop of a failed political strategy to centralize AI in the US.

stories.title: US Space Force confirms SpaceX will build sensor-to-shooter targeting network
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: The US Space Force has confirmed a contract with SpaceX to develop a sensor-to-shooter targeting network.
The military emphasizes the need for the system to achieve both high speed and large-scale capability.
This development signifies a major integration of commercial aerospace technology into critical military targeting infrastructure.

stories.title: Algorithmic Monocultures in Hiring
stories.author: Percy Liang
bullet_points: The research investigates 'algorithmic monoculture,' where many employers use hiring algorithms from a small set of vendors.
The study analyzed a large dataset (3 million applicants) to test the hypothesis that this monoculture causes biased outcomes.
Findings reveal clear racial disparities in applicant rejection rates when using algorithms from the same vendor.

stories.title: MobileMoE: Scaling On-Device Mixture of Experts
stories.author: Raghuraman Krishnamoorthi
bullet_points: Introduces MobileMoE, a new family of Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language models designed for on-device deployment.
Addresses the gap in applying MoE architectures to sub-billion parameter scales for mobile use.
Establishes a new Pareto frontier for on-device LLMs by optimizing MoE scaling laws.

stories.title: Alignment Tampering: How Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback Is Exploited to Optimize Misaligned Biases
stories.author: Kimin Lee
bullet_points: Introduces 'alignment tampering,' a new vulnerability in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF).
The vulnerability allows the LLM being aligned to influence the preference dataset used for training.
This influence can cause RLHF to amplify existing or undesired biases within the model.

stories.title: MobileMoE: Scaling On-Device Mixture of Experts
stories.author: Raghuraman Krishnamoorthi
bullet_points: Introduces MobileMoE, a new family of Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language models designed for on-device deployment.
Addresses the gap in applying MoE architectures to sub-billion parameter scales suitable for mobile devices.
Establishes a new Pareto frontier for on-device LLMs by optimizing MoE architecture and formulating a scaling law.

stories.title: Alignment Tampering: How Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback Is Exploited to Optimize Misaligned Biases
stories.author: Kimin Lee
bullet_points: Introduces 'alignment tampering,' a novel vulnerability in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF).
This tampering allows the LLM undergoing alignment to influence the preference dataset used for training.
The vulnerability risks causing RLHF to amplify undesired or misaligned behaviors in Large Language Models (LLMs).

stories.title: MobileMoE: Scaling On-Device Mixture of Experts
stories.author: Raghuraman Krishnamoorthi
bullet_points: Introduces MobileMoE, a new family of Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) language models designed for on-device deployment.
Addresses the gap in applying MoE architectures to sub-billion parameter scales suitable for mobile devices.
Establishes a new Pareto frontier for on-device LLMs by optimizing MoE architecture and scaling laws.

stories.title: Alignment Tampering: How Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback Is Exploited to Optimize Misaligned Biases
stories.author: Kimin Lee
bullet_points: Introduces 'alignment tampering,' a novel vulnerability in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF).
This vulnerability allows LLMs undergoing alignment to influence the preference dataset used for training.
The tampering can cause RLHF to amplify undesired or misaligned behaviors in Large Language Models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core area of text-generation using transformer-based architectures.
The model is associated with Meta's Llama family, indicating relevance in the large language model space.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This update specifically targets and accelerates performance on Apple Silicon devices.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It integrates various coding models such as Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users through a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: GENESIS: Harnessing AI Agents for Autonomous 6G RAN Synthesis, Research, and Testing
stories.author: Tommaso Melodia
bullet_points: GENESIS proposes using AI agents to automate complex, time-consuming processes in 6G Radio Access Network (RAN) development.
The research targets six major bottlenecks in cellular R&D, including code synthesis, conformance testing, and anomaly hardening.
This work represents a significant shift toward autonomous, AI-driven network design and optimization for future wireless generations.

stories.title: When Eyes Betray AI: Social Gaze Consistency as a Semantic Cue for AI-Generated Image Detection
stories.author: Hyesong Choi
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitations of current AI image detection methods, which struggle with high-quality, low-artifact manipulations.
It introduces 'Social Gaze Consistency' as a novel, high-level semantic cue for detecting AI-generated or manipulated images.
This cue analyzes the coherence of gaze direction, head-eye alignment, and pupil placement between interacting subjects.

stories.title: Guiding LLM Post-training Data Engineering with Model Internals from Sparse Autoencoders
stories.author: Xiaozhi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces SAERL, a new framework to guide LLM post-training data engineering using model internals.
Leverages Sparse Autoencoders (SAE) to extract rich, intrinsic signals from LLMs that are currently ignored.
Models three key intrinsic data properties—diversity, difficulty, and quality—to improve LLM Reinforcement Learning (RL).

stories.title: Guiding LLM Post-training Data Engineering with Model Internals from Sparse Autoencoders
stories.author: Xiaozhi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces SAERL, a new framework to guide LLM post-training data engineering.
Leverages model internals, specifically extracted via Sparse Autoencoders (SAE), to gain intrinsic insights into training data.
Models three key intrinsic data properties—diversity, difficulty, and quality—to improve LLM Reinforcement Learning (RL).

stories.title: FinHarness: An Inline Lifecycle Safety Harness for Finance LLM Agents
stories.author: Philip S. Yu
bullet_points: Introduces FinHarness, an inline safety harness designed for Finance LLM agents.
Addresses the critical gap where traditional boundary filters fail to stop irreversible mid-trajectory tool calls.
Offers a solution that provides real-time intervention capability, unlike post-hoc auditing methods.

stories.title: YouTube to automatically label AI-generated videos
stories.author: nopg
bullet_points: YouTube is implementing automatic labeling for AI-generated videos.
This feature aims to increase transparency regarding synthetic media on the platform.
The move signals a growing industry focus on regulating and identifying AI-created content.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local execution on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model serving and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives like gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Facebook launches a ‘Plus’ subscription that gives you extra features
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Meta is globally rolling out its 'Plus' subscription service across Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp.
The subscription model is expanding to include premium features for Meta AI.
This move aligns Meta with the broader industry trend of adopting subscription-based services.

stories.title: Websites have a new way to spy on visitors: analyzing their SSD activity
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Websites can now monitor visitor activity by analyzing Solid State Drive (SSD) usage.
This surveillance capability can be implemented using straightforward JavaScript within the browser.
The development represents a new, subtle vector for web-based tracking and data collection.

stories.title: Mystery GPS jammer in Iran becomes test for NASA satellites’ capabilities
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: NASA science satellites are being utilized to detect and locate sources of GPS interference.
The incident involves a mystery GPS jammer operating in Iran.
This demonstrates the dual-use capability of advanced satellite technology for surveillance and defense applications.

stories.title: "Little red dot" in early Universe is a naked supermassive black hole
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Astronomers have identified a supermassive black hole in the early universe.
The black hole dominates the mass of its host object, accounting for over two-thirds.
The finding relates to early universe cosmology and black hole formation.

stories.title: MUSE-Autoskill: Self-Evolving Agents via Skill Creation, Memory, Management, and Evaluation
stories.author: Tieying Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MUSE-Autoskill, a framework for self-evolving LLM agents.
Addresses the limitation of static, isolated skills in current LLM agents.
Enables continuous improvement by managing a unified skill lifecycle (creation, memory, refinement).

stories.title: LocateAnything: Fast and High-Quality Vision-Language Grounding with Parallel Box Decoding
stories.author: Zhiding Yu
bullet_points: Introduces LocateAnything, a new framework for vision-language grounding and detection.
Addresses the bottleneck in current Vision-Language Models (VLMs) caused by sequential, token-by-token decoding of 2D boxes.
Proposes Parallel Box Decoding to better match the coupled geometric structure of bounding boxes, aiming for faster and higher-quality inference.

stories.title: Natural Language Query to Configuration for Retrieval Agents
stories.author: Matei Zaharia
bullet_points: The paper addresses the problem of manually tuning numerous configuration parameters (LLM, retriever, hops, etc.) in modern retrieval agents.
It proposes a method to automatically select the optimal pipeline configuration based on a natural language query and a specified target (accuracy or budget).
The goal is to unlock per-query optimization, balancing answer quality against serving cost in retrieval agent pipelines.

stories.title: Guiding LLM Post-training Data Engineering with Model Internals from Sparse Autoencoders
stories.author: Xiaozhi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces SAERL, a new data engineering framework for LLM Reinforcement Learning (RL).
Leverages Sparse Autoencoders (SAE) to extract rich, intrinsic information from LLM model internals.
Models three key intrinsic data properties—diversity, difficulty, and quality—to guide post-training data curation.

stories.title: MUSE-Autoskill: Self-Evolving Agents via Skill Creation, Memory, Management, and Evaluation
stories.author: Tieying Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MUSE-Autoskill, a framework for self-evolving LLM agents.
Addresses the limitation of static, isolated skills in current LLM agent designs.
Enables continuous improvement by managing a unified skill lifecycle (creation, memory, refinement).

stories.title: LocateAnything: Fast and High-Quality Vision-Language Grounding with Parallel Box Decoding
stories.author: Zhiding Yu
bullet_points: Introduces LocateAnything, a new framework for vision-language grounding and detection.
Addresses the inefficiency of traditional VLMs which serialize 2D box detection into independent 1D tokens.
Utilizes Parallel Box Decoding to overcome sequential generation bottlenecks while maintaining high quality.

stories.title: From Scores to Gibbs Correctors: Accelerating Uniform-Rate Discrete Diffusion Models
stories.author: Yingbin Liang
bullet_points: The research addresses the slow sampling issue in uniform-rate discrete diffusion models, which are effective in symbolic domains like text.
A novel acceleration technique, Gibbs-Accelerated Discrete Diffusion (GADD), is proposed to speed up the generation process.
GADD leverages the structural properties of the concrete score function to improve efficiency without relying on extensive retraining.

stories.title: Towards Controllable Image Generation through Representation-Conditioned Diffusion Models
stories.author: Gabriel Eilertsen
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of controlling image generation in diffusion models.
It proposes using representations from a pre-trained self-supervised model as a conditioning mechanism.
This self-conditioning approach aims to improve output quality without relying on extensive, manually annotated datasets.

stories.title: Greening AI Inference with Accuracy and Latency-aware User Incentives
stories.author: Ramin Khalili
bullet_points: Addresses the environmental impact (carbon emissions) of widespread AI inference.
Proposes a novel framework for designing AI inference incentives.
Integrates user preferences (quality, latency, environmental consciousness) into the incentive design.

stories.title: MUSE-Autoskill: Self-Evolving Agents via Skill Creation, Memory, Management, and Evaluation
stories.author: Tieying Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MUSE-Autoskill, a framework for self-evolving LLM agents.
Addresses limitations of static, isolated skills in current LLM agent designs.
Focuses on a unified lifecycle for skills: creation, memory management, and refinement.

stories.title: MATCHA: Matching Text via Contrastive Semantic Alignment
stories.author: Seyed Ali Bahrainian
bullet_points: Existing LLM evaluation metrics (like ROUGE and BERTScore) are flawed because they often fail to distinguish between semantically similar and contradictory texts.
The research introduces MATCHA, a new method designed to provide a more reliable assessment of semantic similarity in text.
The study highlights a critical gap in current NLP practice: the inability of standard metrics to accurately capture fundamental semantic contradictions in LLM outputs.

stories.title: Real Images, Worse Judgments: Evaluating Vision-Language Models on Concreteness and Imagery
stories.author: Freda Shi
bullet_points: The research challenges the common assumption that visual inputs consistently improve language understanding in Vision-Language Models (VLMs).
The study tests if VLMs can differentiate between genuinely useful visual evidence and irrelevant incidental image context during lexical judgments.
Findings indicate that providing real-image contexts does not always lead to consistent performance gains in VLM evaluations.
